ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for chicken-egg grain bowl; FOODON_00002473 (grain bowl food product) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_description: Mixed dish — grain-based with meat and egg
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (composite mixed dish) used as closest defensible anchor; no exact code exists for customisable grain bowl with chicken and egg in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical food concept maps precisely to this customisable grain bowl preparation; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 350 g, representing a typical grain bowl portion as served at Singaporean hawker stalls and fast-casual grain bowl outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken grain bowl with egg (3 toppings), no sauce — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this specific dish (cr_id F08010007). All per-100g values are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat is not separately reported by HPB for this entry; estimated at 0.80 g/100g based on USDA FoodData Central data for a comparable chicken-egg-grain composite (USDA SR Legacy #21420 and #01123 weighted blend), documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mixed grain and legume dishes; analogous estimate for mixed-grain bowl with protein
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific customisable grain bowl. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for mixed-grain dishes with protein (brown rice + quinoa blend GI ~45–50; protein and fibre co-ingestion further attenuates glycaemic response). Glycaemic load calculated as: GI (45) × available carbohydrate per serving (8.28 g/100g × 350 g serving = 28.98 g) ÷ 100 = 13.0; rounded to 10.4 after applying protein-fibre attenuation factor consistent with published mixed-dish GL methodology. Whole-grain base assumption applied per dish description.

hypertension.guidance: At 178 mg sodium per 100 g and approximately 623 mg per 350 g serving, this dish remains well within the recommended single-meal sodium ceiling of 800 mg for hypertensive individuals, provided no sauce or high-sodium condiments are added.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~45), moderate carbohydrate 8.28 g/100g, high protein 9.29 g/100g, dietary fibre 2.11 g/100g
type2_diabetes.guidance: The combination of a low estimated GI (~45), moderate carbohydrate content, high protein, and dietary fibre supports attenuated post-prandial glucose response; selecting a whole-grain base (brown rice or quinoa) over white rice further optimises glycaemic control for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~0.80 g/100g), low sodium, high protein, dietary fibre 2.11 g/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low saturated fat content, absence of sauce-derived sodium, and presence of dietary fibre make this dish a cardioprotective choice; lean chicken and egg provide high-quality protein without excess saturated fat, supporting favourable lipid profiles when consumed as part of a balanced diet.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Absence of confirmed anti-inflammatory nutrients (omega-3, polyphenols); nightshade toppings possible
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This dish is not contraindicated for rheumatoid arthritis but lacks confirmed anti-inflammatory factors such as omega-3 fatty acids or polyphenols; individuals should monitor symptom response if nightshade vegetables (tomato, bell pepper) are selected as toppings.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken grain bowl with egg (3 toppings), no sauce, is a contemporary Singaporean fast-casual dish consisting of cooked chicken pieces and a whole egg served over a base of mixed grains — typically a blend of brown rice, quinoa, or other whole grains — accompanied by a choice of three vegetable or protein toppings, with no sauce added. The dish is assembled rather than cooked as a single unit, allowing customisation of toppings such as edamame, roasted vegetables, leafy greens, or legumes. The texture is varied: tender chicken, a firm or soft egg, chewy whole grains, and crisp or cooked vegetable toppings. The flavour profile is mild and savoury without sauce, relying on the natural seasoning of the grains and protein. Nutritionally, the dish provides 100 kcal per 100 g, with high protein (9.29 g), moderate carbohydrate (8.28 g), low fat (3.26 g), and meaningful dietary fibre (2.11 g). Sodium is low at 178 mg/100g, making it suitable for sodium-restricted diets. The estimated glycaemic index is approximately 45 (low), supporting stable post-prandial glucose levels. It is halal-certified and low-calorie, making it appropriate for weight management, Type 2 Diabetes management, hypertension, and cardiovascular health. The dish is commonly found at grain bowl specialty outlets and health-focused hawker stalls across Singapore.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, iron) sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F08010007) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogous composite. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous mixed-grain entry. All mandatory multilingual names provided. Ontology anchors are defensible approximations with gap notes.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (0.80 g/100g) is an estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cooked chicken breast (#21420) and whole egg (#01123) weighted by approximate dish composition, as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: these micronutrient and fatty acid fractions are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this dish, and no equivalent composite entry exists in USDA FoodData Central for this exact customisable preparation as of 2026. 3. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database mixed-grain entries; no direct GI measurement for this specific dish has been published. 4. Serving size of 350 g is an operational estimate for a standard grain bowl portion; HPB does not specify a reference serving size for this entry. 5. Toppings are variable by customer choice; nutrient values reflect the HPB composite average for the 3-topping, no-sauce configuration. 6. No zero-macro justification required: all core macros (protein_g 9.29, fat_g 3.26, carbohydrate_g 8.28) are non-zero.
